26: from typing import Any
27: 
28: import numpy
29: 
30: from pytools import memoize, memoize_method
31: 
32: 
33: try:
34:     import pycuda._pvt_struct as _struct
35: except ImportError:
36:     import struct as _struct
37: 
38: 
39: @memoize
40: def is_long_64_bit():
41:     return _struct.calcsize("l") == 8
42: 
43: 
44: def dtype_to_ctype(dtype):
45:     if dtype is None:
46:         raise ValueError("dtype may not be None")
47: 
48:     dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
49:     if dtype == numpy.int64:
50:         if is_long_64_bit():
51:             return "long"
52:         else:
53:             return "long long"
54:     elif dtype == numpy.uint64:
55:         if is_long_64_bit():
56:             return "unsigned long"
57:         else:
58:             return "unsigned long long"
59:     elif dtype == numpy.int32:
60:         return "int"
61:     elif dtype == numpy.uint32:
62:         return "unsigned int"
63:     elif dtype == numpy.int16:
64:         return "short int"
65:     elif dtype == numpy.uint16:
66:         return "short unsigned int"
67:     elif dtype == numpy.int8:
68:         return "signed char"
69:     elif dtype == numpy.uint8:
70:         return "unsigned char"
71:     elif dtype == numpy.float32:
72:         return "float"
73:     elif dtype == numpy.float64:
74:         return "double"
75:     elif dtype == numpy.complex64:
76:         return "std::complex<float>"
77:     elif dtype == numpy.complex128:
78:         return "std::complex<double>"
79:     elif dtype == numpy.void:
80:         return "void"
81:     else:
82:         raise ValueError(f"unable to map dtype '{dtype}'")
83: 
84: 
85: class Generable:
86:     def __str__(self):
87:         """Return a single string (possibly containing newlines) representing
88:         this code construct."""
89:         return "\n".join(line.rstrip() for line in self.generate())
90: 
91:     def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
92:         """Generate (i.e. yield) the lines making up this code construct."""
93: 
94:         raise NotImplementedError
95: 
96: 
97: # {{{ declarators
98: 
99: class Declarator(Generable):
100:     def generate(self, with_semicolon=True):
101:         tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
102:         tp_lines = list(tp_lines)
103:         yield from tp_lines[:-1]
104:         sc = ";"
105:         if not with_semicolon:
106:             sc = ""
107:         if tp_decl is None:
108:             yield f"{tp_lines[-1]}{sc}"
109:         else:
110:             yield f"{tp_lines[-1]} {tp_decl}{sc}"
111: 
112:     def get_decl_pair(self):
113:         """Return a tuple ``(type_lines, rhs)``.
114: 
115:         *type_lines* is a non-empty list of lines (most often just a
116:         single one) describing the type of this declarator. *rhs* is the right-
117:         hand side that actually contains the function/array/constness notation
118:         making up the bulk of the declarator syntax.
119:         """
120:         raise NotImplementedError
121: 
122:     def inline(self, with_semicolon=True):
123:         """Return the declarator as a single line."""
124:         tp_lines, tp_decl = self.get_decl_pair()
125:         tp_lines = " ".join(tp_lines)
126:         if tp_decl is None:
127:             return tp_lines
128:         else:
129:             return f"{tp_lines} {tp_decl}"
130: 
131: 
132: class POD(Declarator):
133:     """A simple declarator: The type is given as a :class:`numpy.dtype`
134:     and the *name* is given as a string.
135:     """
136: 
137:     def __init__(self, dtype, name):
138:         self.dtype = numpy.dtype(dtype)
139:         self.name = name
140: 
141:     def get_decl_pair(self):
142:         return [dtype_to_ctype(self.dtype)], self.name
143: 
144:     def struct_maker_code(self, name):
145:         return name
146: 
147:     def struct_format(self):
148:         return self.dtype.char
149: 
150:     def alignment_requirement(self):
151:         return _struct.calcsize(self.struct_format())
152: 
153:     def default_value(self):
154:         return 0
155: 
156:     mapper_method = "map_pod"
157: 
158: 
159: class Value(Declarator):
160:     """A simple declarator: *typename* and *name* are given as strings."""
161: 
162:     def __init__(self, typename, name):
163:         self.typename = typename
164:         self.name = name
165: 
166:     def get_decl_pair(self):
167:         return [self.typename], self.name
168: 
169:     def struct_maker_code(self, data):
170:         raise RuntimeError("named-type values can't be put into structs")
171: 
172:     def struct_format(self):
173:         raise RuntimeError("named-type values have no struct format")
174: 
175:     def default_value(self):
176:         return 0
177: 
178:     mapper_method = "map_value"
179: 
180: 
181: class NestedDeclarator(Declarator):
182:     def __init__(self, subdecl):
183:         self.subdecl = subdecl
184: 
185:     @property
186:     def name(self):
187:         return self.subdecl.name
188: 
189:     def struct_format(self):
190:         return self.subdecl.struct_format()
191: 
192:     def alignment_requirement(self):
193:         return self.subdecl.alignment_requirement()
194: 
195:     def struct_maker_code(self, data):
196:         return self.subdecl.struct_maker_code(data)
197: 
198:     def get_decl_pair(self):
199:         return self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
200: 
201: 
202: class DeclSpecifier(NestedDeclarator):
203:     def __init__(self, subdecl, spec, sep=" "):
204:         NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
205:         self.spec = spec
206:         self.sep = sep
207: 
208:     def get_decl_pair(self):
209:         def add_spec(sub_it):
210:             it = iter(sub_it)
211:             try:
212:                 yield f"{self.spec}{self.sep}{next(it)}"
213:             except StopIteration:
214:                 pass
215: 
216:             yield from it
217: 
218:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
219:         return add_spec(sub_tp), sub_decl
220: 
221: 
222: class NamespaceQualifier(DeclSpecifier):
223:     def __init__(self, namespace, subdecl):
224:         DeclSpecifier.__init__(self, subdecl, namespace, "::")
225: 
226:     mapper_method = "map_namespace_qualifier"
227: 
228: 
229: class Typedef(DeclSpecifier):
230:     def __init__(self, subdecl):
231:         DeclSpecifier.__init__(self, subdecl, "typedef")
232: 
233:     mapper_method = "map_typedef"
234: 
235: 
236: class Static(DeclSpecifier):
237:     def __init__(self, subdecl):
238:         DeclSpecifier.__init__(self, subdecl, "static")
239: 
240:     mapper_method = "map_static"
241: 
242: 
243: class Const(NestedDeclarator):
244:     def get_decl_pair(self):
245:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
246:         return sub_tp, f"const {sub_decl}"
247: 
248:     mapper_method = "map_const"
249: 
250: 
251: class Volatile(NestedDeclarator):
252:     def get_decl_pair(self):
253:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
254:         return sub_tp, f"volatile {sub_decl}"
255: 
256:     mapper_method = "map_volatile"
257: 
258: 
259: class Extern(DeclSpecifier):
260:     def __init__(self, language, subdecl):
261:         self.language = language
262:         super().__init__(subdecl, f'extern "{language}"')
263: 
264:     mapper_method = "map_extern"
265: 
266: 
267: class TemplateSpecializer(NestedDeclarator):
268:     def __init__(self, specializer, subdecl):
269:         self.specializer = specializer
270:         NestedDeclarator.__init__(self, subdecl)
271: 
272:     def get_decl_pair(self):
273:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
274:         sub_tp[-1] = f"{sub_tp[-1]}<{self.specializer}>"
275:         return sub_tp, sub_decl
276: 
277:     mapper_method = "map_template_specializer"
278: 
279: 
280: class MaybeUnused(NestedDeclarator):
281:     def get_decl_pair(self):
282:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
283:         return sub_tp, f"{sub_decl} __attribute__ ((unused))"
284: 
285:     mapper_method = "map_maybe_unused"
286: 
287: 
288: class AlignedAttribute(NestedDeclarator):
289:     """
290:     Assigns an alignment for a definition of a type or an array.
291:     """
292:     def __init__(self, align_bytes, subdecl):
293:         super().__init__(subdecl)
294:         self.align_bytes = align_bytes
295: 
296:     def get_decl_pair(self):
297:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
298:         return sub_tp, f"{sub_decl} __attribute__ ((aligned ({self.align_bytes})))"
299: 
300:     mapper_method = "map_aligned"
301: 
302: 
303: class AlignValueAttribute(NestedDeclarator):
304:     """
305:     Assigns an alignment for value of a pointer.
306: 
307:     This is used for pointers where the user guarantees to the compiler that the
308:     value of the pointer has the alignment stated. :class:`AlignedAttribute` on
309:     the other hand tells the compiler to declare the type or the array with the
310:     given alignment and cannot be used for telling the compiler that an existing
311:     pointer has a certain alignment guarantee.
312:     This attribute is currently supported by clang [1] and Intel [2] and is ignored
313:     by gcc.
314: 
315:     [1]: https://reviews.llvm.org/D4635
316:     [2]: https://www.intel.com/content/www/us/en/develop/documentation/oneapi-dpcpp-cpp-compiler-dev-guide-and-reference/top/compiler-reference/attributes/align-value.html
317:     """
318:     def __init__(self, align_bytes, subdecl):
319:         super().__init__(subdecl)
320:         self.align_bytes = align_bytes
321: 
322:     def get_decl_pair(self):
323:         sub_tp, sub_decl = self.subdecl.get_decl_pair()
324:         return (sub_tp,

422: class Struct(Declarator):
423:     """A structure declarator."""
424: 
425:     def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None, pad_bytes=0):
426:         """Initialize the structure declarator.
427:         *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
428:         name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
429:         padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
430:         *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
431:         """
432:         self.tpname = tpname
433:         self.fields = fields
434:         self.declname = declname
435:         self.pad_bytes = pad_bytes
436: 
437:     def get_decl_pair(self):
438:         def get_tp():
439:             if self.tpname is not None:
440:                 yield f"struct {self.tpname}"
441:             else:
442:                 yield "struct"
443:             yield "{"
444:             for f in self.fields:
445:                 for f_line in f.generate():
446:                     yield f"  {f_line}"
447:             if self.pad_bytes:
448:                 yield f"  unsigned char _cgen_pad[{self.pad_bytes}];"
449:             yield "} " + self.struct_attributes()
450:         return get_tp(), self.declname
451: 
452:     def alignment_requirement(self):
453:         return max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in self.fields)
454: 
455:     def struct_attributes(self):
456:         return ""
457: 
458:     mapper_method = "map_struct"

461: class GenerableStruct(Struct):
462:     def __init__(self, tpname, fields, declname=None,
463:             align_bytes=None, aligned_prime_to=None):
464:         """Initialize a structure declarator.
465:         *tpname* is the name of the structure, while *declname* is the
466:         name used for the declarator. *pad_bytes* is the number of
467:         padding bytes added at the end of the structure.
468:         *fields* is a list of :class:`Declarator` instances.
469: 
470:         *align_bytes* is an integer that causes the structure to be
471:         padded to an integer multiple of itself.
472:         *aligned_prime_to* is a list of integers.
473:         If the resulting structure's size
474:         is ``s``, then ``s//align_bytes`` will be made prime to all
475:         numbers in *aligned_prime_to*. (Sounds obscure? It's needed
476:         for avoiding bank conflicts in CUDA programming.)
477:         """
478:         if aligned_prime_to is None:
479:             aligned_prime_to = []
480: 
481:         format = "".join(f.struct_format() for f in fields)
482:         bytes = _struct.calcsize(format)
483: 
484:         natural_align_bytes = max(f.alignment_requirement() for f in fields)
485:         if align_bytes is None:
486:             align_bytes = natural_align_bytes
487:         elif align_bytes < natural_align_bytes:
488:             from warnings import warn
489:             warn("requested struct alignment smaller than natural alignment",
490:                  stacklevel=2)
491: 
492:         self.align_bytes = align_bytes
493: 
494:         padded_bytes = ((bytes + align_bytes - 1) // align_bytes) * align_bytes
495: 
496:         def satisfies_primality(n):
497:             import math
498:             for p in aligned_prime_to:
499:                 if math.gcd(n, p) != 1:
500:                     return False
501:             return True
502: 
503:         while not satisfies_primality(padded_bytes // align_bytes):
504:             padded_bytes += align_bytes
505: 
506:         Struct.__init__(self, tpname, fields, declname, padded_bytes - bytes)
507: 
508:         if self.pad_bytes:
509:             self.format = format + f"{self.pad_bytes}x"
510:             self.bytes = padded_bytes
511:         else:
512:             self.format = format
513:             self.bytes = bytes
514: 
515:         assert _struct.calcsize(self.format) == self.bytes
516: 
517:     # until nvcc bug is fixed
518:     # def struct_attributes(self):
519:     #     return "__attribute__ ((packed))"
520: 
521:     def alignment_requirement(self):
522:         return self.align_bytes
523: 
524:     def make(self, **kwargs):
525:         """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
526:         :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
527:         in *kwargs*.
528:         """
529:         return self._maker()(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
530: 
531:     def make_with_defaults(self, **kwargs):
532:         """Build a binary, packed representation of *self* in a
533:         :class:`str` instance with members set to the values specified
534:         in *kwargs*.
535: 
536:         Unlike :meth:`make`, not all members have to occur in *kwargs*.
537:         """
538:         return self._maker(with_defaults=True)(_struct.pack, **kwargs)
539: 
540:     @memoize_method
541:     def _maker(self, with_defaults=False):
542:         def format_arg(f):
543:             if with_defaults:
544:                 return f"{f.name}={f.default_value()!r}"
545:             else:
546:                 return f.name
547: 
548:         code = "lambda pack, {}: pack({}, {})".format(
549:                 ", ".join(format_arg(f) for f in self.fields),
550:                 repr(self.struct_format()),
551:                 ", ".join(f.struct_maker_code(f.name) for f in self.fields))
552:         return eval(code)
553: 
554:     def struct_format(self):
555:         """Return the format of the struct as digested by the :mod:`struct`
556:         module.
557:         """
558:         return self.format
559: 
560:     def __len__(self):
561:         """Return the number of bytes occupied by this struct."""
562:         return self.bytes
563: 
564:     mapper_method = "map_generable_struct"
565:

567: class Enum(Generable):
568:     """An enum-like class for Python that can generate an equivalent C-level
569:     declaration. Does not work within the usual "declarator" framework
570:     because it uses macros to define values, and a separate typedef to define the
571:     value type.
572: 
573:     .. versionadded:: 2013.2
574: 
575:     To use, derive from this class, and define the following things:
576: 
577:     .. attribute:: c_name
578:     .. attribute:: dtype
579:     .. attribute:: c_value_prefix
580: 
581:         A (usually upper-case) prefix to be used as a prefix to the names defined
582:         on the Python side.
583:     .. attribute:: VALUE
584: 
585:         Any class attribute with an all-upper-case name is taken to be as an
586:         enum value.
587:     """
588: 
589:     c_name: str
590:     dtype: "numpy.dtype[Any]"
591:     c_value_prefix: str
592: 
593:     @classmethod
594:     def get_flag_names_and_values(cls):
595:         return [(name, getattr(cls, name))
596:                 for name in sorted(dir(cls))
597:                 if name[0].isupper()]
598: 
599:     @classmethod
600:     def get_c_defines_lines(cls):
601:         return [
602:                 f"#define {cls.c_value_prefix}{flag_name} {value}"
603:                 for flag_name, value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()]
604: 
605:     @classmethod
606:     def get_c_defines(cls):
607:         """Return a string with C defines corresponding to these constants.
608:         """
609: 
610:         return "\n".join(cls.get_c_defines_lines())
611: 
612:     @classmethod
613:     def get_c_typedef_line(cls):
614:         """Returns a typedef to define this enum in C."""
615: 
616:         from pyopencl.tools import dtype_to_ctype  # pylint: disable=import-error
617:         return f"typedef {dtype_to_ctype(cls.dtype)} {cls.c_name};"
618: 
619:     @classmethod
620:     def get_c_typedef(cls):
621:         return f"\n\n{cls.get_c_typedef_line()}\n\n"
622: 
623:     @classmethod
624:     def generate(cls):
625:         yield cls.get_c_typedef_line()
626:         yield from cls.get_c_defines_lines()
627: 
628:     @classmethod
629:     def stringify_value(cls, val):
630:         """Return a string description of the flags set in *val*."""
631: 
632:         return "|".join(
633:                 flag_name
634:                 for flag_name, flag_value in cls.get_flag_names_and_values()
635:                 if val & flag_value)
636:

1044:     else:
1045:         return Block(contents)

1048: class LiteralLines(Generable):
1049:     def __init__(self, text):
1050:         # accommodate pyopencl syntax highlighting
1051:         if text.startswith("//CL//"):
1052:             text = text[6:]
1053: 
1054:         if not text.startswith("\n"):
1055:             raise ValueError("expected newline as first character "
1056:                     "in literal lines")
1057: 
1058:         lines = text.split("\n")
1059:         while lines[0].strip() == "":
1060:             lines.pop(0)
1061:         while lines[-1].strip() == "":
1062:             lines.pop(-1)
1063: 
1064:         if lines:
1065:             base_indent = 0
1066:             while lines[0][base_indent] in " \t":
1067:                 base_indent += 1
1068: 
1069:             for line in lines[1:]:
1070:                 if line[:base_indent].strip():
1071:                     raise ValueError("inconsistent indentation")
1072: 
1073:         self.lines = [line[base_indent:] for line in lines]
1074: 
1075:     def generate(self):
1076:         yield from self.lines
1077: 
1078:     mapper_method = "map_literal_lines"
1079: 
1080: 
1081: class LiteralBlock(LiteralLines):
1082:     def generate(self):
1083:         yield "{"
1084:         for line in self.lines:
1085:             yield f"  {line}"
1086:         yield "}"

1098: class IfDef(Module):
1099:     """
1100:     Class to represent IfDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
1101:     :param condition: the condition in IfDef
1102:     :param iflines: the block of code inside the if [an array of type Generable]
1103:     :param elselines: the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]
1104:     """
1105:     def __init__(self, condition, iflines, elselines):
1106:         ifdef_line = Line(f"#ifdef {condition}")
1107:         if len(elselines):
1108:             elselines.insert(0, Line("#else"))
1109:         endif_line = Line("#endif")
1110:         lines = [ifdef_line, *iflines, *elselines, endif_line]
1111:         super().__init__(lines)
1112: 
1113:     mapper_method = "map_ifdef"

1116: class IfNDef(Module):
1117:     """
1118:     Class to represent IfNDef-Else-EndIf construct for the C preprocessor.
1119:     :param condition: the condition in IfNDef
1120:     :param ifndeflines: the block of code inside the if not
1121:         [an array of type Generable]
1122:     :param elselines: the block of code inside the else [an array of type Generable]
1123:     """
1124:     def __init__(self, condition, ifndeflines, elselines):
1125:         ifndefdef_line = Line(f"#ifndef {condition}")
1126:         if len(elselines):
1127:             elselines.insert(0, Line("#else"))
1128:         lines = [ifndefdef_line, *ifndeflines, *elselines, Line("#endif")]
1129:         super().__init__(lines)
1130: 
1131:     mapper_method = "map_ifndef"

1134: class PrivateNamespace(Block):
1135:     def get_namespace_name(self):
1136:         try:
1137:             import hashlib
1138:             checksum = hashlib.md5()
1139:         except ImportError:
1140:             # for Python << 2.5
1141:             import md5
1142:             checksum = md5.new()
1143: 
1144:         for c in self.contents:
1145:             for line in c.generate():
1146:                 checksum.update(line.encode("utf-8"))
1147: 
1148:         return "private_namespace_"+checksum.hexdigest()
1149: 
1150:     def generate(self):
1151:         yield "namespace "+self.get_namespace_name()
1152:         yield "{"
1153:         for item in self.contents:
1154:             for item_line in item.generate():
1155:                 yield f"  {item_line}"
1156:         yield "}"
1157:         yield ""
1158:         yield f"using namespace {self.get_namespace_name()};"
1159:

31: class ASTMapper:
32:     def __call__(self, node, *args, **kwargs):
33:         """Dispatch *node* to its corresponding mapper method. Pass on
34:         ``*args`` and ``**kwargs`` unmodified.
35: 
36:         This method is intended as the top-level dispatch entry point and may
37:         be overridden by subclasses to present a different/more convenient
38:         interface. :meth:`rec` on the other hand is intended as the recursive
39:         dispatch method to be used to recurse within mapper method
40:         implementations.
41:         """
42: 
43:         try:
44:             method = getattr(self, node.mapper_method)
45:         except AttributeError:
46:             return self.handle_unsupported(
47:                     node, *args, **kwargs)
48: 
49:         return method(node, *args, **kwargs)
50: 
51:     def handle_unsupported(self, node, *args, **kwargs):
52:         """Mapper method that is invoked for node subclasses for which a mapper
53:         method does not exist in this mapper.
54:         """
55: 
56:         raise UnsupportedNodeError(
57:                 "{} cannot handle nodes of type {}".format(
58:                     type(self), type(node)))
59: 
60:     rec = __call__

26: def dtype_to_cltype(dtype):
27:     if dtype is None:
28:         raise ValueError("dtype may not be None")
29: 
30:     dtype = np.dtype(dtype)
31:     if dtype == np.int64:
32:         return "long"
33:     elif dtype == np.uint64:
34:         return "ulong"
35:     elif dtype == np.int32:
36:         return "int"
37:     elif dtype == np.uint32:
38:         return "uint"
39:     elif dtype == np.int16:
40:         return "short"
41:     elif dtype == np.uint16:
